old_exact: FILED IN THE OFFICE OF THE CLERK OF SUPREME COURT DECEMBER 28, 2023 STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA
evidence: The filing date/header text appears in the PDF only at the top of the document as part of the page header (lines 1-5). In the database, this exact text is incorrectly inserted after the attorney line (after 'Julie A. Lawyer, State's Attorney, Bismarck, ND, for respondent and appellee.'), where it should not appear. The source PDF shows the text should flow directly from the attorney line to the running header 'Everett v. State / No. 20230192' (after a form-feed). This is an OCR/text extraction ingestion error.
note: This is not a simple OCR character error but rather a structural misplacement where the page furniture (filing information) was relocated to the middle of the opinion body instead of appearing only at the top as document metadata.

evidence: The phrase 'include tilling soil and applying' appears in the authoritative PDF source (lines 307 of pdftotext output) immediately after 'Duties may', but in the DB text it is displaced to the end of the sentence before 'harvested products', breaking the grammatical structure. The DB text reads '...loading include tilling soil and applying harvested products' with the action words severed from their object. The correct version has the natural phrase structure restored: 'Duties may include tilling soil and applying fertilizers;'
note: This is a transcription/ingestion error where a phrase was split and rearranged during PDF-to-text processing. The error appears in paragraph 28 in the Job Service report description of 'Farmworkers and Laborers, Crop, Nursery, and Greenhouse' job duties.

evidence: PDF: line 119 has '                                    III', line 120 is blank, line 121 has '[¶6] The order deferring imposition...'. DB places III after the ¶6 paragraph instead of before it, violating the opinion's section structure (I before ¶1-2, II before ¶3-5, III before ¶6-7).
